---
title: "Calculating the accessibility and spatial availability of jobs (Census Tract level)"
author: "Bruno Santos & Antonio Paez"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  tufte::tufte_handout:
    citation_package: natbib
    latex_engine: xelatex
  tufte::tufte_html: default
  tufte::tufte_book:
    citation_package: natbib
    latex_engine: xelatex
bibliography: mybibfile.bib
link-citations: yes
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

```{r clean-workspace, include=FALSE}
# cleaning objects from the workspace 
rm(list = ls())
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# layout configuration 
library(tufte)
library(knitr)
options(htmltools.dir.version = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

```{r load-packages}
library(dplyr) # data manipulation
library(here) # file path management
library(tidyr) # reshaping data
library(Hmisc) # weighted quantiles
library(survey) # survey-weighted models
library(svydiags) # diagnostics for survey models
library(svyEffects) # average marginal effects
library(ggplot2) # plotting
library(sf) # spatial data handling
library(tmap) # thematic maps
library(estimatr) # test iv robustness 
```

# Introduction 

After calculating accessibility and spatial filters, this Rmarkdown processes survey data from the National Survey on Transport Poverty, from the Mobilizing Justice Partnership, links it to the derived accessibility measures and estimates their causal effects on perceived employment outcomes. It uses a Two-Stage Residual Inclusion (2SRI) approach where spatial filters  serve as instruments for endogenous calculated accessibility variables. We estimate both linear and ordinal models, and the results are used to derive accessibility thresholds for equity‑deserving populations.

# Suggested Readings

-   Bastiaanssen, Jeroen, Daniel Johnson, and Karen Lucas. "The relationship between individual employment probabilities and accessibility to matching jobs: A study of the Netherlands." Transportation Research Part A: Policy and Practice 193 (2025): 104398.
- Terza, Joseph V., Anirban Basu, and Paul J. Rathouz. "Two-stage residual inclusion estimation: addressing endogeneity in health econometric modeling." Journal of health economics 27.3 (2008): 531-543.

# Reading Data and Preprocessing 

Reading the National Survey on Transport Poverty (NSTP). Reading the main file:

```{r}
files_address <- paste0(here(),"/data-raw/MJ-Survey/ns_data_re.csv")
mjsurvey <- read.csv(files_address, header = TRUE)
mjsurvey_backup <- mjsurvey
```

Reading the household‑level data: 

```{r}
files_address <- paste0(here(),"/data-raw/MJ-Survey/Household_Members_Data_2025-04-16.csv")
mjsurvey_household <- read.csv(files_address, header = TRUE)
```

Reading a file with spatial keys to connect the different spatial units: 

```{r}
files_address <- paste0(here(),"/data-raw/input/census_spatial_keys.csv")

keys <- read.csv(files_address, header = TRUE, 
               colClasses = c("character", "character","character", "character", "character",
                              "character","character"))
```

Defining the folder with the study area measures:

```{r}
study_area_folder <- paste0(here::here(),"/data-raw/output/GTAH/")
```

Reading calculated job accessibility and spatial filter variables:

```{r read-accessibility}
# Folder  
measures_folder <- paste0(study_area_folder,"/CT/spatial-filter-measures/")

# Read file
Accessibilities <- read.csv(paste0(measures_folder, "spatial_filters.csv"), 
               colClasses = c("character", "character", 
                              "numeric","numeric",
                              "numeric","numeric", 
                              "numeric","numeric" )) %>% 
  mutate(PwMode_label = case_when(
    PwMode_label == "Car-motor" ~ "Car_motor",
    TRUE ~ PwMode_label ))
```

Read the spatial geometries (dissemination areas, census tracts, census subdivisions, and population centres): 

```{r}
directory_spatial_files <- paste0(study_area_folder,"/spatial-files/") # Update this address if necessary

# DAs
dissemination_areas <- st_read(paste0(directory_spatial_files, "dissemination_areas.gpkg"))

# CTs
census_tracts <- st_read(paste0(directory_spatial_files, "census_tracts.gpkg"))
census_tracts <- nngeo::st_remove_holes(census_tracts)

# CSD
census_subdivisions <- st_read(paste0(directory_spatial_files, "census_subdivisions.gpkg"))
census_subdivisions <- nngeo::st_remove_holes(census_subdivisions)

# CD
census_divisions <- st_read(paste0(directory_spatial_files, "census_divisions.gpkg"))
census_divisions <- nngeo::st_remove_holes(census_divisions)

# POPCENTRE
population_centres <- st_read(paste0(directory_spatial_files, "population_centres.gpkg"))
```

Add labels for the six regional municipalities (PCD codes):

```{r}
census_subdivisions <- census_subdivisions %>% 
  mutate(PCD = substr(CDUID, 0, 4),
         pcd_label = case_when(PCD == "3518" ~ "Durham", 
                               PCD == "3519" ~ "York", 
                               PCD == "3520" ~ "Toronto", 
                               PCD == "3521" ~ "Peel", 
                               PCD == "3524" ~ "Halton", 
                               PCD == "3525" ~ "Hamilton"))

census_divisions <- census_divisions %>% 
  mutate(PCD = substr(CDUID, 0, 4),
         pcd_label = case_when(PCD == "3518" ~ "Durham", 
                               PCD == "3519" ~ "York", 
                               PCD == "3520" ~ "Toronto", 
                               PCD == "3521" ~ "Peel", 
                               PCD == "3524" ~ "Halton", 
                               PCD == "3525" ~ "Hamilton"))
```
 
Setting folders to export results and figures:

```{r directory-export}
# Measures
diretorio_export <- paste0(study_area_folder,"/CT/standards-measures-per-person/")
if(!dir.exists(diretorio_export)) dir.create(diretorio_export, recursive = TRUE)

# Figures
diretorio_export_images <- paste0(study_area_folder,"/CT/standards-figures-per-person/")
if(!dir.exists(diretorio_export_images)){
  dir.create(diretorio_export_images, recursive = TRUE)
  dir.create(paste0(diretorio_export_images, "/linear/all/"), recursive = TRUE)
  dir.create(paste0(diretorio_export_images, "/linear/sig/"), recursive = TRUE) 
  dir.create(paste0(diretorio_export_images, "/ordinal/"), recursive = TRUE)
}
```

# Pre‑processing Survey Responses

Four key outcomes are derived from survey questions measured on a 0 to 100 scale. For each, we create a Likert version weighted quintiles (fexp_final as weights):

- DeclineEmployment: _"Declining employment opportunities because of transport situation"_ (higher = worse; from _pe_agreeWithDeclineEmploymentOpportunitiesDueToTransport_)
- FewJobs: _"Too few jobs in my ideal commuting time"_ (higher = worse; from _pe_tooFewJobsInMyIdealCommutingTime_)
- TravelTooLong: "Travel to work too long" (higher = worse; from _pe_tooLongToTravelToWork_)
- EasyToGo: "Easily reach work" (higher = better; _pe_easilyReachWork_)

Also extract transit‑related perceptions (satisfaction, wait time, reliability, etc.) and forced car ownership to perform some tests (some of these variables were not used for the models). 

```{r}
pcd_codes <- c("3518", # Durham
          "3519", # York
          "3520" ,  # Toronto
          "3521", # Peel
          "3524", # Halton
          "3525" # Hamilton
          ) 

mjsurvey_study_area <- mjsurvey %>% 
  filter(substr(DAUID_ADIDU, 1, 4) %in% pcd_codes)

# Declining employment opportunities because of transport situation 
LS_DeclineEmployment <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(fexp_final, X_activePersonId,pe_agreeWithDeclineEmploymentOpportunitiesDueToTransport) %>% 
  filter(!pe_agreeWithDeclineEmploymentOpportunitiesDueToTransport %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(DeclineEmployment = as.numeric(pe_agreeWithDeclineEmploymentOpportunitiesDueToTransport)) %>% 
  filter(DeclineEmployment >= 0) %>%
  mutate(DeclineEmployment_Likert = 
           cut(DeclineEmployment,
               breaks = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(DeclineEmployment, weights = fexp_final, probs = c(0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, 1)), 
               #breaks = c(0,20,40,60,80,100), 
               labels = c("Most Positive", "Positive", "Neutral", "Negative", "Most Negative"),
               include.lowest = TRUE), 
         DeclineEmployment_Likert = factor(DeclineEmployment_Likert, 
                                              levels = c("Most Negative", "Negative", "Neutral","Positive", "Most Positive"),
             ordered = TRUE))

# Few job availability with ideal commute 
LS_FewJobs <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(fexp_final, X_activePersonId,pe_tooFewJobsInMyIdealCommutingTime, p_mainOccupation) %>% 
  filter(!pe_tooFewJobsInMyIdealCommutingTime %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(FewJobs = as.numeric(pe_tooFewJobsInMyIdealCommutingTime)) %>% 
  filter(FewJobs >= 0) %>%
  mutate(FewJobs_Likert = 
           cut(FewJobs,
               breaks = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(FewJobs, weights = fexp_final, probs = c(0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, 1)), 
               #breaks = c(0,20,40,60,80,100), 
               labels = c("Most Positive", "Positive", "Neutral", "Negative", "Most Negative"),
               include.lowest = TRUE), 
         FewJobs_Likert = factor(FewJobs_Likert, 
                                              levels = c("Most Negative", "Negative", "Neutral","Positive", "Most Positive"),
             ordered = TRUE))

# Travel to work too long 
LS_TravelTooLong <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(fexp_final, X_activePersonId,pe_tooLongToTravelToWork) %>% 
  filter(!pe_tooLongToTravelToWork %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(TravelTooLong = as.numeric(pe_tooLongToTravelToWork)) %>% 
  filter(TravelTooLong >= 0) %>%
  mutate(TravelTooLong_Likert = 
           cut(TravelTooLong,
               breaks = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(TravelTooLong, weights = fexp_final, probs = c(0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, 1)), 
               #breaks = c(0,20,40,60,80,100), 
               labels = c("Most Positive", "Positive", "Neutral", "Negative", "Most Negative"),
               include.lowest = TRUE), 
         TravelTooLong_Likert = factor(TravelTooLong_Likert, 
                                              levels = c("Most Negative", "Negative", "Neutral","Positive", "Most Positive"),
             ordered = TRUE))

# Easy to go work
LS_EasyToGo <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select( fexp_final,  X_activePersonId,pe_easilyReachWork, p_fixedWorkplaceOutsideHome, p_mainOccupation) %>% 
  filter(!pe_easilyReachWork %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(EasyToGo = as.numeric(pe_easilyReachWork)) %>% 
  filter(EasyToGo >= 0) %>%
  mutate(EasyToGo_Likert = 
           cut(EasyToGo,
               breaks = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(EasyToGo, weights = fexp_final, probs = c(0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, 1)), 
               #breaks = c(0,20,40,60,80,100), 
               labels = c("Most Negative", "Negative", "Neutral","Positive", "Most Positive"),
               include.lowest = TRUE), 
         EasyToGo_Likert = factor(EasyToGo_Likert, 
                                              levels = c("Most Negative", "Negative", "Neutral","Positive", "Most Positive"),
             ordered = TRUE))

# Public Transit Variables

## TransportSatisfaction (***)
LS_TransportSatisfaction <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_transportSatisfaction) %>% 
  filter(!pe_transportSatisfaction %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(TransportSatisfaction = as.numeric(pe_transportSatisfaction)) %>% 
  filter(TransportSatisfaction >= 0)

## TransitWaitTime (***)
LS_TransitWaitTime <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_tooLongwaitTimeForTransit) %>% 
  filter(!pe_tooLongwaitTimeForTransit %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(TransitWaitTime = as.numeric(pe_tooLongwaitTimeForTransit)) %>% 
  filter(TransitWaitTime >= 0)

## TransitReliable (***)
LS_TransitReliable <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_thinkPublicTransportIsReliable) %>% 
  filter(!pe_thinkPublicTransportIsReliable %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(TransitReliable = as.numeric(pe_thinkPublicTransportIsReliable)) %>% 
  filter(TransitReliable >= 0)

## TransitPractical (***)
LS_TransitPractical <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_thinkPublicTransportIsPractical) %>% 
  filter(!pe_thinkPublicTransportIsPractical %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(TransitPractical = as.numeric(pe_thinkPublicTransportIsPractical)) %>% 
  filter(TransitPractical >= 0)

## TransitTimeSave (***)
LS_TransitTimeSave <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_thinkPublicTransportIsTimeSaving) %>% 
  filter(!pe_thinkPublicTransportIsTimeSaving %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(TransitTimeSave = as.numeric(pe_thinkPublicTransportIsTimeSaving)) %>% 
  filter(TransitTimeSave >= 0)

## Forced to have a car
LS_ForcedCar <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_agreeWithFeelForcedToOwnCar) %>% 
  filter(!pe_agreeWithFeelForcedToOwnCar %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(ForcedCar = as.numeric(pe_agreeWithFeelForcedToOwnCar)) %>% 
  filter(ForcedCar >= 0)


# Public Transit Variables

## Work by Transit (****)
LS_WorkbyTransit <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,p_averageTimesGoWorkplaceTransit) %>% 
  filter(!p_averageTimesGoWorkplaceTransit %in% ("")) %>% 
  mutate(WorkbyTransit = factor(p_averageTimesGoWorkplaceTransit, levels= c("almostNever","lessThan1TimeWeek", "1TimeWeek",
                                                  "2TimesWeek", "3TimesWeek",
                                                  "4TimesWeek" , "5TimesWeekOrMore"), ordered = TRUE)) %>% 
  mutate(WorkbyTransit_count = case_when(
      p_averageTimesGoWorkplaceTransit == "almostNever" ~ 0,
      p_averageTimesGoWorkplaceTransit == "lessThan1TimeWeek" ~ 0,
      p_averageTimesGoWorkplaceTransit == "1TimeWeek" ~ 1,
      p_averageTimesGoWorkplaceTransit == "2TimesWeek" ~ 2,
      p_averageTimesGoWorkplaceTransit == "3TimesWeek" ~ 3,
      p_averageTimesGoWorkplaceTransit == "4TimesWeek" ~ 4,
      p_averageTimesGoWorkplaceTransit == "5TimesWeekOrMore" ~ 5)) 


## TransportSatisfaction (***)
LS_TransportSatisfaction <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_transportSatisfaction) %>% 
  filter(!pe_transportSatisfaction %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(TransportSatisfaction = as.numeric(pe_transportSatisfaction)) %>% 
  filter(TransportSatisfaction >= 0)

## TransitWaitTime (***)
LS_TransitWaitTime <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_tooLongwaitTimeForTransit) %>% 
  filter(!pe_tooLongwaitTimeForTransit %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(TransitWaitTime = as.numeric(pe_tooLongwaitTimeForTransit)) %>% 
  filter(TransitWaitTime >= 0)

## TransitReliable (***)
LS_TransitReliable <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_thinkPublicTransportIsReliable) %>% 
  filter(!pe_thinkPublicTransportIsReliable %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(TransitReliable = as.numeric(pe_thinkPublicTransportIsReliable)) %>% 
  filter(TransitReliable >= 0)

## TransitPractical (***)
LS_TransitPractical <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_thinkPublicTransportIsPractical) %>% 
  filter(!pe_thinkPublicTransportIsPractical %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(TransitPractical = as.numeric(pe_thinkPublicTransportIsPractical)) %>% 
  filter(TransitPractical >= 0)

## TransitTimeSave (***)
LS_TransitTimeSave <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_thinkPublicTransportIsTimeSaving) %>% 
  filter(!pe_thinkPublicTransportIsTimeSaving %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(TransitTimeSave = as.numeric(pe_thinkPublicTransportIsTimeSaving)) %>% 
  filter(TransitTimeSave >= 0)

## Forced to have a car
LS_ForcedCar <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(X_activePersonId,pe_agreeWithFeelForcedToOwnCar) %>% 
  filter(!pe_agreeWithFeelForcedToOwnCar %in% c("na","")) %>% 
  mutate(ForcedCar = as.numeric(pe_agreeWithFeelForcedToOwnCar)) %>% 
  filter(ForcedCar >= 0)
```

We derive the labour force status from _p_mainOccupation_, selecting as employed those who answered to be working full‑time or part‑time, gig workers, and self‑employed. People who answered to be retired and unable to work are excluded from labour force:
```{r}
# Labour force (employed = 1)
occ_char <- as.character(mjsurvey_study_area$p_mainOccupation)

has_target <- sapply(strsplit(occ_char, ","), function(x) {
any(trimws(x) %in% c("fullTimeWorker", "partTimeWorker", "gigWorker",
"selfEmploymentWorker", "jobSeeker", "unemployed"))})

labour_force <- mjsurvey_study_area[has_target, ]

exclusion_cases <- sapply(strsplit(occ_char, ","), function(x) {
any(trimws(x) %in% c("retired", "unableToWork"))})

labour_force <- labour_force[!exclusion_cases, ]

labour_force$employment_status <- sapply(strsplit(labour_force$p_mainOccupation, ","), function(x) {
if (any(trimws(x) %in% c("fullTimeWorker", "partTimeWorker", "gigWorker", "selfEmploymentWorker"))) 1 else 0})

labour_force <- labour_force %>%
  dplyr::select(c(X_activePersonId, p_mainOccupation, employment_status))
```

Combine all outcomes into one data frame:

```{r}
outcomes_std <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% dplyr::select(interview_id, X_activePersonId, fexp_final) %>% 
  left_join(LS_DeclineEmployment %>% dplyr::select(-fexp_final) , by = "X_activePersonId") %>% 
  left_join(LS_FewJobs %>% dplyr::select(-fexp_final) , by = "X_activePersonId") %>% 
  left_join(LS_TravelTooLong %>% dplyr::select(-fexp_final), by = "X_activePersonId") %>% 
  left_join(LS_EasyToGo %>% dplyr::select(-fexp_final), by = "X_activePersonId") %>%
  left_join(LS_WorkbyTransit, by = "X_activePersonId") %>%
  left_join(LS_TransportSatisfaction, by = "X_activePersonId") %>%
  left_join(LS_TransitWaitTime, by = "X_activePersonId") %>%
  left_join(LS_TransitReliable, by = "X_activePersonId") %>%
  left_join(LS_TransitPractical, by = "X_activePersonId") %>%
  left_join(LS_TransitTimeSave, by = "X_activePersonId") %>%
  left_join(LS_ForcedCar, by = "X_activePersonId") %>% 
  left_join(labour_force, by = "X_activePersonId")
```

Obtain demographic, socioeconomic, and transport‑related controls variables: income categories, education, driver license, transit pass, indigenous/racialized status, gender, age, household size, car/bike ownership, etc. Also create mode groups (Car‑motor, Transit, Active) and binary indicators.

```{r}
control_var <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(interview_id, 
                X_activePersonId,
                fexp_final, 
                DAUID_ADIDU,
                t_householdCarNumber,
                t_householdBikeNumber,
                t_driverLicense,
                h_houseOwnership,
                t_monthlyOrAnnualTransitPass,
                t_transitIsAvailable,
                t_useParatransitServices, 
                s_genderIdentity, # 
                s_sexualOrientation, 
                s_highestEducationLevel, # 
                s_bornInCanada, 
                s_firstNationsMetisOrInuit, 
                s_ethnicity, # 
                s_canadianStatus, # 
                s_longTermConditionsDifficultySeeing, 
                s_longTermConditionsDifficultyHearing, 
                s_longTermConditionsDifficultyWalking, 
                s_longTermConditionsDifficultyLearning,           
                s_emotionalPsychologicalMentalConditions, 
                pe_preferredMode, pe_doesFrequentModeMatchPreferred, pe_mostFrequentMode,
                s_age,
                hh_size, 
                hh_householdIncome) %>% 
  mutate(hh_income = case_when(hh_householdIncome == "000000_009999" ~ "Less than $30,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "010000_019999" ~ "Less than $30,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "020000_029999" ~ "Less than $30,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "030000_039999" ~ "$30,000 to $60,000",
                               
                               hh_householdIncome == "040000_049999" ~ "$30,000 to $60,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "050000_059999" ~ "$30,000 to $60,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "060000_069999" ~ "$60,000 to $90,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "070000_079999" ~ "$60,000 to $90,000", 
                               
                               hh_householdIncome == "080000_089999" ~ "$60,000 to $90,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "090000_119999" ~ "$90,000 to $120,000", 
                               
                               hh_householdIncome == "120000_149999" ~ "$120,000 to $150,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "150000_179999" ~ "$150,000 to $180,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "180000_209999" ~ "$180,000 to $210,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "210000_999999" ~ "More than $210,000", 
    ),
    hh_income = factor(hh_income, levels = c("Less than $30,000",
                                             "$30,000 to $60,000", 
                                             "$60,000 to $90,000", 
                                             "$90,000 to $120,000", 
                                             "$150,000 to $180,000", 
                                             "$180,000 to $210,000", 
                                             "More than $210,000")), 
    
    hh_income4 = case_when(hh_householdIncome == "000000_009999" ~ "Less than $40,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "010000_019999" ~ "Less than $40,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "020000_029999" ~ "Less than $40,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "030000_039999" ~ "Less than $40,000", 
                               
                               hh_householdIncome == "040000_049999" ~ "$40,000 to $80,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "050000_059999" ~ "$40,000 to $80,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "060000_069999" ~ "$40,000 to $80,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "070000_079999" ~ "$40,000 to $80,000", 
                               
                               hh_householdIncome == "080000_089999" ~ "$80,000 to $120,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "090000_119999" ~ "$80,000 to $120,000", 
                               
                               hh_householdIncome == "120000_149999" ~ "More than $120,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "150000_179999" ~ "More than $120,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "180000_209999" ~ "More than $120,000", 
                               hh_householdIncome == "210000_999999" ~ "More than $120,000", 
    ),
    hh_income4 = factor(hh_income4, levels = c("Less than $40,000",
                                             "$40,000 to $80,000", 
                                             "$80,000 to $120,000", 
                                             "More than $120,000")), 
    
    education_level = case_when(s_highestEducationLevel %in% c("noCertificateDiplomaDegree", "highSchoolDiploma") ~ "High school or less",
      s_highestEducationLevel %in% c("collegeCegepNonUniversityCertificateDiploma", "apprenticeshipTradesCertificateDiploma") ~ "Post-secondary non-university",
      s_highestEducationLevel %in% c("universityBelowBachelor", "universityBachelorOrAbove") ~ "University"),
    
    education_level = factor(education_level),
    
    DriverLicense = case_when(
      t_driverLicense == "yes" ~ "Yes",
     TRUE ~ "No"
    ),
    DriverLicense = factor(DriverLicense, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    TransitPass = case_when(
      t_monthlyOrAnnualTransitPass == "yes" ~ "Yes",
     TRUE ~ "No"
    ),
    TransitPass = factor(TransitPass, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    TransitAvailable = case_when(
      t_transitIsAvailable == "yes" ~ "Yes",
     TRUE ~ "No"
    ),
    TransitAvailable = factor(TransitAvailable, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    Paratransit = case_when(
      t_useParatransitServices == "yes" ~ "Yes",
     TRUE ~ "No"
    ),
    Paratransit = factor(Paratransit, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    Indigenous = case_when(
      s_firstNationsMetisOrInuit == "yes" ~ "Yes",
     TRUE ~ "No"
    ),
    Indigenous = factor(Indigenous, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    Non_White = case_when(
      s_ethnicity == "white"  ~ "No",
     TRUE ~ "Yes"
    ),
    Non_White = factor(Non_White, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    Indigenous_or_racialized = case_when(
      (s_ethnicity == "white" | s_firstNationsMetisOrInuit == "yes") ~ "No",
     TRUE ~ "Yes"
    ),
    Indigenous_or_racialized = factor(Indigenous_or_racialized, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    Temporary_or_refugee = case_when(
      s_canadianStatus %in% c("temporaryResident", "refugeeAsylumSeeker") ~ "Yes",
     TRUE ~ "No"
     ),
    Temporary_or_refugee = factor(Temporary_or_refugee, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    houseOwnership = factor(h_houseOwnership, levels = c("Rent","Owner")),
    
    LongTermConditions = case_when(
      s_longTermConditionsDifficultySeeing %in% c("often", "always") |  
        s_longTermConditionsDifficultyHearing %in% c("often", "always") |  
        s_longTermConditionsDifficultyWalking %in% c("often", "always") |  
        s_longTermConditionsDifficultyLearning %in% c("often", "always") |  
        s_emotionalPsychologicalMentalConditions %in% c("often", "always") ~ "Yes",
     TRUE ~ "No"
    ),
    LongTermConditions = factor(LongTermConditions, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    Immigrant = case_when(
      s_bornInCanada == "yes" ~ "Yes",  # Born in Canada = not immigrant
     TRUE ~ "No"
    ),
    Immigrant = factor(Immigrant, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    Woman = case_when(
      s_genderIdentity %in% c("woman", "transWoman") ~ "Yes",
      TRUE ~ "No"
    ),
    Woman = factor(Woman, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    Woman_or_genderdiverse = case_when(
      !s_genderIdentity %in% c("man") ~ "Yes",
      TRUE ~ "No"
    ),
    Woman_or_genderdiverse = factor(Woman_or_genderdiverse, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    
    twoSTQI = case_when(
      s_genderIdentity %in% c("woman", "man") ~ "No",
      TRUE ~ "Yes"
    ),
    twoSTQI = factor(twoSTQI, levels = c("No", "Yes")), 
    
    mostFrequentMode = case_when(
      pe_doesFrequentModeMatchPreferred == "yes" ~ pe_preferredMode,
      pe_doesFrequentModeMatchPreferred == "no" ~ pe_mostFrequentMode),
    
     Transport_mode = case_when(
       mostFrequentMode %in% c("carDriver", "carPassenger", "motorcycle", "scooter","taxiOrRidehailingApp") ~ "Car-motor",
       mostFrequentMode %in% c("bus", "metro", "regionalTrain", "tram","lightRapidTransit") ~ "Transit",
       mostFrequentMode %in% c("bike", "bikeSharing", "assitedBike") ~ "Bike",
       mostFrequentMode %in% c("walkOrWheeling") ~ "Walk"), 
       
    Transport_mode = factor(Transport_mode, levels = c("Car-motor", "Transit", "Bike", "Walk")),
    
    
      Transport_3mode = case_when(
       mostFrequentMode %in% c("carDriver", "carPassenger", "motorcycle", "scooter","taxiOrRidehailingApp") ~ "Car-motor",
       mostFrequentMode %in% c("bus", "metro", "regionalTrain", "tram","lightRapidTransit") ~ "Transit",
       mostFrequentMode %in% c("bike", "bikeSharing", "assitedBike","walkOrWheeling") ~ "Active"), 
       
    Transport_3mode = factor(Transport_3mode, levels = c("Car-motor", "Transit", "Active")),
    
    Transit_user = case_when(Transport_mode == "Transit" ~ "Yes",
                             !Transport_mode == "Transit" ~ "No"),
    
    Transit_user = factor(Transit_user, levels = c("No","Yes")),
    
    Transit_user_bin = case_when(Transport_mode == "Transit" ~ 1,
                             !Transport_mode == "Transit" ~ 0),

    Car_user_bin = case_when(Transport_mode == "Car-motor" ~ 1,
                             !Transport_mode == "Car-motor" ~ 0), 
    
    Active_user_bin = case_when(Transport_mode %in% c("Walk","Bike") ~ 1,
                             !Transport_mode %in% c("Walk","Bike") ~ 0), 
    
    Active_user = case_when(Transport_mode %in% c("Walk","Bike") ~ "Yes",
                             !Transport_mode %in% c("Walk","Bike") ~ "No"),
   
     Active_user = factor(Active_user, levels = c("No","Yes")),

    Match_mode = case_when(pe_doesFrequentModeMatchPreferred == "yes" ~ "Yes",
                           pe_doesFrequentModeMatchPreferred == "no" ~ "No"),
    Match_mode = factor(Match_mode, levels = c("No","Yes")), 
    
    CarNumber = t_householdCarNumber, 
    BikeNumber = t_householdBikeNumber,
    Age = s_age, 
    age_group = case_when(Age < 15 ~ "<= 15", 
                           Age >= 15 &  Age < 25 ~ "15 - 25",
                           Age >= 25 &  Age < 35 ~ "25 - 35",
                           Age >= 35 &  Age < 45 ~ "35 - 45",
                           Age >= 45 &  Age < 55 ~ "45 - 55",
                           Age >= 55 &  Age < 65 ~ "55 - 65",
                           Age >= 65 ~ "65+"), 
     age_group = factor(age_group), 
     car_number_group = case_when(CarNumber == 0 ~ "0", 
                           CarNumber == 1 ~ "1", 
                           CarNumber > 1 ~ "2 or more"), 
     car_number_group = factor(car_number_group)) %>%
  dplyr::select(-c(t_driverLicense, t_monthlyOrAnnualTransitPass, 
            t_transitIsAvailable, t_useParatransitServices,
            s_firstNationsMetisOrInuit, s_ethnicity,
            s_canadianStatus, s_longTermConditionsDifficultySeeing,
            s_longTermConditionsDifficultyHearing, s_longTermConditionsDifficultyWalking,
            s_longTermConditionsDifficultyLearning, s_emotionalPsychologicalMentalConditions,
            s_bornInCanada, 
            s_genderIdentity, pe_preferredMode, pe_doesFrequentModeMatchPreferred, pe_mostFrequentMode))
```

Number of children under 10: 

```{r}
child_number <- mjsurvey_household %>% 
  mutate(people = 1) %>%
  group_by(interview_id) %>% 
  summarise(Child_under_10 = sum(people[age <= 10]))


control_var <- control_var %>% 
  left_join(child_number, by = "interview_id")
```

Reshape accessibility table from long to wide table (each mode and spatial filter become separate columns so we can join them to the individual level data):

```{r}
accessibilities_wide <- Accessibilities %>%
  rename(SA = SA_im, 
         HT = HT_im,
         SA_pp = SA_pp_im) %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = CTUID,
              names_from = PwMode_label, 
              values_from = c(SA, Sf_dist_SA, HT, Sf_dist_HT, SA_pp, Sf_dist_SA_pp),
              names_sep = "_")
```

Calculate the population density based on the DAs: 

```{r}
dissemination_areas <- dissemination_areas %>% 
  mutate(Pop_density = Population/LANDAREA) %>%
  mutate(DAUID = as.numeric(DAUID))
```

Creating the population centre varible ("Rural", "Small", "Medium", or "Large"):

```{r}
mjsurvey_study_area_pc <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("h_geography_x", "h_geography_y"), crs = st_crs(census_tracts)) %>% 
  sf::st_join(population_centres %>% dplyr::select(pc_class, pc_type), largest = TRUE) %>% 
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  dplyr::select(c(interview_id, DAUID_ADIDU, pc_type, pc_class)) %>% 
  mutate(pc_class = case_when(is.na(pc_class) ~ "Rural",
                              TRUE ~ pc_class), 
         pc_class = factor(pc_class, levels = c("Rural", "Small", "Medium", "Large")),
         pc_type = case_when(is.na(pc_type) ~ "Rural",
                              TRUE ~ pc_type), 
         pc_type = factor(pc_type, levels = c("Rural", "Secondary core", "Fringe", "Core"))) 
```
Combine all geographic and accessibility variables: 

```{r}
keys <- keys %>% 
  mutate(DAUID = as.numeric(DAUID))

geo_vars <- mjsurvey_study_area %>% 
  dplyr::select(interview_id,X_activePersonId,  
                DAUID_ADIDU) %>% 
  rename(DAUID = DAUID_ADIDU) %>% 
  left_join(keys, by = "DAUID") %>% 
  left_join(accessibilities_wide, by = "CTUID") %>%
  left_join(dissemination_areas %>% dplyr::select(DAUID, Pop_density),
            by = "DAUID") %>% 
  left_join(mjsurvey_study_area_pc, by = "interview_id")
```

Join all controls and outcomes: 

```{r}
respondents <- geo_vars %>% 
  left_join(control_var, by = c("interview_id", "X_activePersonId")) %>% 
  left_join(outcomes_std %>% dplyr::select(-fexp_final), by = c("interview_id", "X_activePersonId")) %>%
  filter(PCD %in% pcd_codes) %>% 
  mutate(fexp_scaled = fexp_final / mean(fexp_final, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% # Scaled survey weights
  filter(!is.na(CTUID), 
         !is.na(SA_pp_Active), 
         !is.na(SA_pp_Transit), 
         !is.na(hh_income))
```

Save the respondent data with all controls and outcomes for reproducibility: 

```{r}
write.csv(respondents, paste0(diretorio_export, "respondents_processed.csv"), row.names=FALSE)
```

# Regressions: first stage

In this study we assume that the calculated accessibility is potentially endogenous to perceived employment outcomes. We use the Two-stage residual inclusion (2SRI) to address this potential endogeneity. First, we regress the accessibility indicators based on the spatial filters (instrumental variables) and the control variables. Then, we add the residuals of these first stage regressions to the estimation of the outcome, using the original accessibility indicators and controls. 

We use `svydesign` with weights `fexp_final` (no clustering) to create our survey design: 

```{r}
design_respondents <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,  # No clustering
  weights = ~fexp_final,
  data = respondents)
```

## Transit IV

Creating the first stage for transit accessibility:

```{r}
stage1_transit_SA_pp <- svyglm(SA_pp_Transit ~ SA_pp_Active   +
SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit   +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
SA_pp_Car_motor +
## Transport Variables
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
## Socio and demographic variables
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size  +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class, design = design_respondents)

summary(stage1_transit_SA_pp, diagnostics = TRUE)
```

Wald test for the instrument significance:

```{r}
wald_transit <- regTermTest(stage1_transit_SA_pp, ~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit)
wald_transit
```

## Car IV

Creating the first stage for Car-motorized accessibility:

```{r}
stage1_car_SA_pp <- svyglm(`SA_pp_Car_motor` ~ SA_pp_Active   +
SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
SA_pp_Transit   +
SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor +
## Transport Variables
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
## Socio and demographic variables
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size  +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class, design = design_respondents)

summary(stage1_car_SA_pp, diagnostics = TRUE)
```

Wald test for the instrument significance:

```{r}
wald_car <- regTermTest(stage1_car_SA_pp, ~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor)
wald_car
```

## Active IV

Creating the first stage for Active accessibility:

```{r}
stage1_active_SA_pp <- svyglm(`SA_pp_Active` ~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active   +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
SA_pp_Transit   +
SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
SA_pp_Car_motor +
## Transport Variables
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
## Socio and demographic variables
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size  +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class, design = design_respondents)

summary(stage1_active_SA_pp)
```

Wald test for the instrument significance:

```{r}
wald_active <- regTermTest(stage1_active_SA_pp, ~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active)
wald_active
```

Generate predicted values and residuals from first stage: 

```{r}
respondents <- respondents %>%
  mutate(SA_pp_Transit_IV = predict(stage1_transit_SA_pp, newdata = ., type = "response"),
    SA_pp_Car_IV = predict(stage1_car_SA_pp, newdata = ., type = "response"),
    SA_pp_Active_IV = predict(stage1_active_SA_pp, newdata = ., type = "response"),
    SA_pp_Transit_Res_IV = SA_pp_Transit - SA_pp_Transit_IV,  
    SA_pp_Car_Res_IV = `SA_pp_Car_motor` - SA_pp_Car_IV,  
    SA_pp_Active_Res_IV = SA_pp_Active - SA_pp_Active_IV)
```

# Regressions: second stage - linear

Although the main objective of this study is to identify the effect of accessibility on perceived employment outcomes in their categorized (Likert) form, we also estimate linear regressions based on the continuum outcomes to compare with them to the categorical models. The linear models use the predicted accessibility from the first stage (`SA_pp_ * _IV`) as regressors, in a classic 2SLS (two-stage least squares) approach. The same set of control variables is included.

Define a reusable formula: 

```{r}
########################
##### 3 Modes Case #####
########################

stage2_linear_formula <- as.formula(Outcome ~
## Spatial Availability variables
SA_pp_Active_IV   +
SA_pp_Active_IV:Active_user +
SA_pp_Transit_IV   +
SA_pp_Transit_IV:Transit_user +
SA_pp_Car_IV +
## Transport Variables
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
## Socio and demographic variables
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size  +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class
)

```

## Few Jobs

```{r}
# Create survey design
design_FewJobs <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,  # No clustering
  weights = ~fexp_final,
  data = respondents %>% 
  filter(!is.na(FewJobs)))
```

```{r}
FewJobs_formula_3mode_lm_SA <- formula(update(stage2_linear_formula, FewJobs ~ .))

FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA <- svyglm(FewJobs_formula_3mode_lm_SA,
                                   design = design_FewJobs)
summary(FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA)
```

```{r}
summary(FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA$model)
```

```{r}
null_deviance <- FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA$null.deviance
model_deviance <- FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA$deviance
r_squared <- 1 - (model_deviance / null_deviance)
r_squared
```

For each outcome, we fit the model, then iteratively remove outliers with absolute studentised residuals > 2 (using `svystdres`) to improve robustness. This is repeated up to 10 times.

```{r}
formula_current <- FewJobs_formula_3mode_lm_SA
model_current <- FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA
design_current <- design_FewJobs
```

```{r}
max_iter <- 10
iter <- 0
outliers_removed <- 0

while (iter < max_iter) {
  

  # survey-weighted standardized residuals
  stdres_obj <- svystdres(model_current)
  stdres_vals <- stdres_obj$stdresids

  
  #cookD <- svyCooksD(model_current)
  #n <- nrow(design_current$variables)
  #cd_threshold <- quantile(cookD, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)

  
  influential <- which(abs(stdres_vals) > 2)
  
  # h <- svyhat(model_current)
  # influential <- which(h > 3 * mean(h) | abs(stdres_vals) > 2)
  # 
  #influential <- which(cookD > cd_threshold)
  
  if (length(influential) == 0) {
    cat("No more outliers with |stdres| > 2. Stopping.\n")
    break
  }
  
  cat("Iteration", iter + 1, ": removing", length(influential), "outliers\n")
  
  keep <- !(seq_len(nrow(design_current$variables)) %in% influential)
  design_new <- subset(design_current, keep)
  
  # refit the model
  model_new <- svyglm(formula_current,
                      design = design_new)
  
  design_current <- design_new
  model_current <- model_new
  iter <- iter + 1
  outliers_removed <- outliers_removed + length(influential)
}

cat("Total outliers removed:", outliers_removed, "\n")
```

After cleaning, we compute R2 (1 - deviance/null.deviance) and variance inflation factors (VIF) to check multicollinearity. We also run an instrumental variable diagnostic using iv_robust (from `estimatr`) to assess weak instruments.

```{r}
null_deviance <- model_current$null.deviance
model_deviance <- model_current$deviance
r_squared <- 1 - (model_deviance / null_deviance)
r_squared
```

```{r}
summary(model_current)
```

```{r}
car::vif(model_current)
```

```{r}
svydiags::svystdres(model_current, doplot=TRUE)
```

```{r}
summary(svydiags::svystdres(model_current)$stdresids)
```

```{r}
FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean <- model_current
FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean_design <- design_current
```

Test to identify weak instruments:

```{r}
iv_model_fj <- iv_robust(FewJobs ~ 
SA_pp_Active   +
SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
SA_pp_Transit   +
SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
SA_pp_Car_motor +
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class 
| Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active   +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit   +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor +
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class, data = FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean_design$variables,
                      weights = fexp_final,
                      diagnostics = TRUE)

summary(iv_model_fj)
```

```{r}
# morans_fj <- FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean$data %>% 
#   dplyr::select("interview_id") %>% 
#   left_join(respondents %>% dplyr::select("interview_id", "Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active", "Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit", "Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor",  "CTUID"), by = "interview_id") %>% 
#   left_join(census_tracts, by = "CTUID") %>% 
#   st_as_sf(crs = st_crs(census_tracts))
# 
# morans_fj$residuals <- svystdres(FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean)$stdresids
# 
# wieghts <- morans_fj %>% 
# poly2nb(queen = TRUE) %>% 
# nb2listw(style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE)
# 
# moran.test(morans_fj$residuals, listw = wieghts, zero.policy = TRUE)
```

## Travel Too Long

```{r}
# Create survey design
design_TravelTooLong <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,  # No clustering
  weights = ~fexp_final,
  data = respondents %>% 
  filter(!is.na(TravelTooLong))) 
```

```{r}
TravelTooLong_formula_3mode_lm_SA <- formula(update(stage2_linear_formula, TravelTooLong ~ .))

TravelTooLong_3mode_lm_SA <- svyglm(TravelTooLong_formula_3mode_lm_SA,
                                   design = design_TravelTooLong)

summary(TravelTooLong_3mode_lm_SA)
```

```{r}
null_deviance <- TravelTooLong_3mode_lm_SA$null.deviance
model_deviance <- TravelTooLong_3mode_lm_SA$deviance
r_squared <- 1 - (model_deviance / null_deviance)
r_squared
```

```{r}
formula_current <- TravelTooLong_formula_3mode_lm_SA
model_current <- TravelTooLong_3mode_lm_SA
design_current <- design_TravelTooLong
```

```{r}
max_iter <- 10
iter <- 0
outliers_removed <- 0

while (iter < max_iter) {
  
  #survey-weighted standardized residuals
  stdres_obj <- svystdres(model_current)
  stdres_vals <- stdres_obj$stdresids
  
  influential <- which(abs(stdres_vals) > 2)
  
  # h <- svyhat(model_current)
  # influential <- which(h > 3 * mean(h) | abs(stdres_vals) > 2)
  
  if (length(influential) == 0) {
    cat("No more outliers with |stdres| > 2. Stopping.\n")
    break
  }
  
  cat("Iteration", iter + 1, ": removing", length(influential), "outliers\n")
  
  keep <- !(seq_len(nrow(design_current$variables)) %in% influential)
  design_new <- subset(design_current, keep)
  
  # refit the model
  model_new <- svyglm(formula_current,
                      design = design_new)
  
  design_current <- design_new
  model_current <- model_new
  iter <- iter + 1
  outliers_removed <- outliers_removed + length(influential)
}

cat("Total outliers removed:", outliers_removed, "\n")
```

```{r}
summary(model_current)
```

```{r}
null_deviance <- model_new$null.deviance
model_deviance <- model_new$deviance
r_squared <- 1 - (model_deviance / null_deviance)
r_squared
```

```{r}
car::vif(model_current)
```

```{r}
svydiags::svystdres(model_current, doplot=TRUE)
```

```{r}
summary(svydiags::svystdres(model_current)$stdresids)
```

```{r}
TravelTooLong_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean <- model_current
TravelTooLong_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean_design <- design_current
```

Test to identify weak instruments:

```{r}
iv_model_ttl <- iv_robust(TravelTooLong ~ 
SA_pp_Active   +
SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
SA_pp_Transit   +
SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
SA_pp_Car_motor +
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class 
| Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active   +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit   +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor +
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class, data = TravelTooLong_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean_design$variables,
                      weights = fexp_final,
                      diagnostics = TRUE)

summary(iv_model_ttl)
```

## Easy to Reach

To what extent do you agree with the following? “With the transport options available to me, I can easily participate/reach the following destinations:” Work

```{r}
# Create survey design
design_EasyToGo <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,  # No clustering
  weights = ~fexp_final,
  data = respondents %>% 
  filter(!is.na(EasyToGo)))
```

```{r}
EasyToGo_formula_3mode_lm_SA <- formula(update(stage2_linear_formula, EasyToGo ~ .))

EasyToGo_3mode_lm_SA <- svyglm(EasyToGo_formula_3mode_lm_SA,
                                   design = design_EasyToGo)
summary(EasyToGo_3mode_lm_SA)
```

```{r}
null_deviance <- EasyToGo_3mode_lm_SA$null.deviance
model_deviance <- EasyToGo_3mode_lm_SA$deviance
r_squared <- 1 - (model_deviance / null_deviance)
r_squared
```

```{r}
formula_current <- EasyToGo_formula_3mode_lm_SA
model_current <- EasyToGo_3mode_lm_SA
design_current <- design_EasyToGo
```

```{r}
max_iter <- 10
iter <- 0
outliers_removed <- 0

while (iter < max_iter) {
  
  # survey-weighted standardized residuals
  stdres_obj <- svystdres(model_current)
  stdres_vals <- stdres_obj$stdresids
  
  influential <- which(abs(stdres_vals) > 2)
  
  # h <- svyhat(model_current)
  # 
  # influential <- which(h > 3 * mean(h) | abs(stdres_vals) > 2)
  
  if (length(influential) == 0) {
    cat("No more outliers with |stdres| > 2. Stopping.\n")
    break
  }
  
  cat("Iteration", iter + 1, ": removing", length(influential), "outliers\n")
  
  keep <- !(seq_len(nrow(design_current$variables)) %in% influential)
  design_new <- subset(design_current, keep)
  
  # refit the model
  model_new <- svyglm(formula_current,
                      design = design_new)
  
  design_current <- design_new
  model_current <- model_new
  iter <- iter + 1
  outliers_removed <- outliers_removed + length(influential)
}

cat("Total outliers removed:", outliers_removed, "\n")
```

```{r}
summary(model_current)
```

```{r}
null_deviance <- model_new$null.deviance
model_deviance <- model_new$deviance
r_squared <- 1 - (model_deviance / null_deviance)
r_squared
```

```{r}
car::vif(model_current)
```

```{r}
svydiags::svystdres(model_current, doplot=TRUE)
```

```{r}
summary(svydiags::svystdres(model_current)$stdresids)
```

```{r}
EasyToGo_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean <- model_current
EasyToGo_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean_design <- design_current
```

Test to identify weak instruments:

```{r}
iv_model_etg <- iv_robust(EasyToGo ~ 
SA_pp_Active   +
SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
SA_pp_Transit   +
SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
SA_pp_Car_motor +
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class 
| Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active   +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit   +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor +
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class, data = EasyToGo_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean_design$variables,
                      weights = fexp_final,
                      diagnostics = TRUE)

summary(iv_model_etg)
```

## Decline Employment

```{r}
# Create survey design
design_DeclineEmployment <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,  # No clustering
  weights = ~fexp_final,
  data = respondents %>% 
  filter(!is.na(DeclineEmployment), !is.na(employment_status)))
```

```{r}
DeclineEmployment_formula_3mode_lm_SA <- formula(update(stage2_linear_formula, DeclineEmployment ~ .))

DeclineEmployment_linear_3modes <- svyglm(DeclineEmployment_formula_3mode_lm_SA,
                                   design = design_DeclineEmployment)

summary(DeclineEmployment_linear_3modes)
```

```{r}
null_deviance <- DeclineEmployment_linear_3modes$null.deviance
model_deviance <- DeclineEmployment_linear_3modes$deviance
r_squared <- 1 - (model_deviance / null_deviance)
r_squared
```

```{r}
formula_current <- DeclineEmployment_formula_3mode_lm_SA
model_current <- DeclineEmployment_linear_3modes
design_current <- design_DeclineEmployment
```

```{r}
max_iter <- 10
iter <- 0
outliers_removed <- 0

while (iter < max_iter) {
  
  # survey-weighted standardized residuals
  stdres_obj <- svystdres(model_current)
  stdres_vals <- stdres_obj$stdresids
  
  influential <- which(abs(stdres_vals) > 2)
  
  # h <- svyhat(model_current)
  # influential <- which(h > 3 * mean(h) | abs(stdres_vals) > 2)
  
  if (length(influential) == 0) {
    cat("No more outliers with |stdres| > 2. Stopping.\n")
    break
  }
  
  cat("Iteration", iter + 1, ": removing", length(influential), "outliers\n")
  
  keep <- !(seq_len(nrow(design_current$variables)) %in% influential)
  design_new <- subset(design_current, keep)
  
  # refit the model
  model_new <- svyglm(formula_current,
                      design = design_new)
  
  design_current <- design_new
  model_current <- model_new
  iter <- iter + 1
  outliers_removed <- outliers_removed + length(influential)
}

cat("Total outliers removed:", outliers_removed, "\n")
```

```{r}
summary(model_current)
```

```{r}
null_deviance <- model_new$null.deviance
model_deviance <- model_new$deviance
r_squared <- 1 - (model_deviance / null_deviance)
r_squared
```

```{r}
car::vif(model_current)
```

```{r}
svydiags::svystdres(model_current, doplot=TRUE)
```

```{r}
summary(svydiags::svystdres(model_current)$stdresids)
```

```{r}
DeclineEmployment_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean <- model_current
DeclineEmployment_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean_design <- design_current
```

Test to identify weak instruments:

```{r}
iv_model_de <- iv_robust(DeclineEmployment ~ 
SA_pp_Active   +
SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
SA_pp_Transit   +
SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
SA_pp_Car_motor +
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class 
| Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active   +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit   +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor +
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size +
hh_income4 +
Pop_density +
pc_class, data = DeclineEmployment_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean_design$variables,
                      weights = fexp_final,
                      diagnostics = TRUE)

summary(iv_model_de)
```

# Regressions: second stage - ordinal  

The perceived outcomes are on ordinal scale (Likert variables) and now we estimate cumulative link models (`svyolr`) using the 2SRI approach, in which we include the first‑stage residuals as additional regressors to correct for endogeneity, so the residuals absorb the endogenous variation.

The ordinal formula is:

```{r}
ordinal_SA_pp_3mode_formula <- as.formula(Outcome ~ 
                                # Spatial Availability variables
SA_pp_Active   +
SA_pp_Active:Active_user +
SA_pp_Transit   +
SA_pp_Transit:Transit_user +
SA_pp_Car_motor +
# Transport variables
CarNumber +
BikeNumber +
Match_mode +
TransitPass +
TransitAvailable +
# Socio and demographic variables
Indigenous_or_racialized +
Temporary_or_refugee +
LongTermConditions +
Woman_or_genderdiverse +
Age +
hh_size +
hh_income4 +
# Environmental
Pop_density +
pc_class +
# # Causal-effect
SA_pp_Active_Res_IV +
SA_pp_Transit_Res_IV +
SA_pp_Car_Res_IV)
```

Creating a new dataframe with a reduced number of variables:

```{r}
respondents_filtered <- respondents %>%
dplyr::select(SA_pp_Active, SA_pp_Transit, SA_pp_Car_motor, 
              Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active, Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit, Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor, 
              SA_Active, SA_Transit, SA_Car_motor, Transit_user_bin, Active_user_bin, 
               Transit_user, Active_user, TransitPass,  Car_user_bin, pc_class, hh_income4, Transport_3mode,
CarNumber, BikeNumber, Match_mode, Indigenous_or_racialized,
Temporary_or_refugee, LongTermConditions, Woman_or_genderdiverse,
Age, hh_size, hh_income, Pop_density, SA_pp_Active_Res_IV,
SA_pp_Transit_Res_IV, SA_pp_Car_Res_IV, Transit_user,
FewJobs, FewJobs_Likert,
TravelTooLong, TravelTooLong_Likert,
EasyToGo, EasyToGo_Likert,
DeclineEmployment, DeclineEmployment_Likert,
fexp_final, education_level, TransitAvailable, PCD, employment_status)
```

## Few jobs: Likert

Prior to estimation, all continuous predictors (accessibility, population density, residuals) are standardised (mean 0, sd 1) to make coefficients comparable:

```{r}
complete_data_fj <- respondents_filtered %>% 
  filter(!is.na(FewJobs))  %>%
  mutate(fexp_normalized = fexp_final / mean(fexp_final, na.rm = TRUE), 
         SA_pp_Active_unscaled = SA_pp_Active,
         SA_pp_Car_motor_unscaled = SA_pp_Car_motor,
         SA_pp_Transit_unscaled = SA_pp_Transit)

vars_to_scale <- c("SA_pp_Active", "SA_pp_Transit", "SA_pp_Car_motor",
  "Pop_density", 
  "SA_pp_Active_Res_IV", "SA_pp_Transit_Res_IV", "SA_pp_Car_Res_IV")

complete_data_scaled_fj <- complete_data_fj %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(vars_to_scale), 
                function(x) {
                  scaled <- scale(x)
                  return(as.numeric(scaled))
                }))

scale_centers_fj <- sapply(vars_to_scale, function(var) {
  attr(scale(complete_data_fj[[var]]), "scaled:center")
})

scale_scales_fj <- sapply(vars_to_scale, function(var) {
  attr(scale(complete_data_fj[[var]]), "scaled:scale")
})
```

```{r}
design_ordinal_norm_fj <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,
  weights = ~fexp_normalized,
  data = complete_data_scaled_fj)
```

```{r}
FewJobs_formula_3mode_ordinal_SA <- formula(update(ordinal_SA_pp_3mode_formula, FewJobs_Likert ~ .))

FewJobs_ordinal_3modes <- svyolr(FewJobs_formula_3mode_ordinal_SA,
                                   design = design_ordinal_norm_fj)

summary(FewJobs_ordinal_3modes)
```

```{r}
car::vif(FewJobs_ordinal_3modes)
```

Significance of transit accessibility (using `regTermTest`):

```{r}
survey::regTermTest(FewJobs_ordinal_3modes, "SA_pp_Transit")
```

We also compute the correlation between each spatial filter and the outcome (as a simple bivariate check): 

```{r}
active_cor <- svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active + FewJobs, design = design_ordinal_norm_fj)$cors[2]
transit_cor <-svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit + FewJobs, design = design_ordinal_norm_fj)$cors[2]
car_cor <-svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor + FewJobs, design = design_ordinal_norm_fj)$cors[2]

filters <- c("Active", "Transit", "Car")
values <- c(active_cor, transit_cor, car_cor)
fewjobscorr <- data.frame(Filter = filters, "FewJobs" = values)
fewjobscorr
```

## Travel Too Long: Likert

```{r}
complete_data_ttg <- respondents_filtered %>% 
  filter(!is.na(TravelTooLong)) %>%
  mutate(fexp_normalized = fexp_final / mean(fexp_final, na.rm = TRUE), 
         SA_pp_Active_unscaled = SA_pp_Active,
         SA_pp_Car_motor_unscaled = SA_pp_Car_motor,
         SA_pp_Transit_unscaled = SA_pp_Transit)

complete_data_scaled_ttg <- complete_data_ttg %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(vars_to_scale), 
                function(x) {
                  scaled <- scale(x)
                  return(as.numeric(scaled))
                }))

scale_centers_ttg <- sapply(vars_to_scale, function(var) {
  attr(scale(complete_data_ttg[[var]]), "scaled:center")
})

scale_scales_ttg <- sapply(vars_to_scale, function(var) {
  attr(scale(complete_data_ttg[[var]]), "scaled:scale")
})
```

```{r}
design_ordinal_norm_ttg <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,
  weights = ~fexp_normalized,
  data = complete_data_scaled_ttg)
```

```{r}
TravelTooLong_formula_3mode_ordinal_SA <- formula(update(ordinal_SA_pp_3mode_formula, TravelTooLong_Likert ~ .))

TravelTooLong_ordinal_3modes <- svyolr(TravelTooLong_formula_3mode_ordinal_SA,
                                   design = design_ordinal_norm_ttg)

summary(TravelTooLong_ordinal_3modes)
```

We also compute the correlation between each spatial filter and the outcome (as a simple bivariate check): 

```{r}
active_cor <- svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active + TravelTooLong, design = design_ordinal_norm_ttg)$cors[2]
transit_cor <- svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit + TravelTooLong, design = design_ordinal_norm_ttg)$cors[2]
car_cor <- svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor + TravelTooLong, design = design_ordinal_norm_ttg)$cors[2]

filters <- c("Active", "Transit", "Car")
values <- c(active_cor, transit_cor, car_cor)
traveltoolongcorr <- data.frame(Filter = filters, "TravelTooLong" = values)

traveltoolongcorr
```

## Easy to Reach: Likert

```{r}
complete_data_etg <- respondents_filtered %>% 
  filter(!is.na(EasyToGo)) %>%
  mutate(fexp_normalized = fexp_final / mean(fexp_final, na.rm = TRUE), 
         SA_pp_Active_unscaled = SA_pp_Active,
         SA_pp_Car_motor_unscaled = SA_pp_Car_motor,
         SA_pp_Transit_unscaled = SA_pp_Transit)

complete_data_scaled_etg <- complete_data_etg %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(vars_to_scale), 
                function(x) {
                  scaled <- scale(x)
                  return(as.numeric(scaled))
                }))

scale_centers_etg <- sapply(vars_to_scale, function(var) {
  attr(scale(complete_data_etg[[var]]), "scaled:center")
})

scale_scales_etg <- sapply(vars_to_scale, function(var) {
  attr(scale(complete_data_etg[[var]]), "scaled:scale")
})
```

```{r}
design_ordinal_norm_etg <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,
  weights = ~fexp_normalized,
  data = complete_data_scaled_etg)
```

```{r}
EasyToGo_formula_3mode_ordinal_SA <- formula(update(ordinal_SA_pp_3mode_formula, EasyToGo_Likert ~ .))

EasyToGo_ordinal_3modes <- svyolr(EasyToGo_formula_3mode_ordinal_SA,
                                   design = design_ordinal_norm_etg)

summary(EasyToGo_ordinal_3modes)
```

```{r}
survey::regTermTest(EasyToGo_ordinal_3modes, "SA_pp_Transit")
```

```{r}
active_cor <- svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active + EasyToGo, design = design_ordinal_norm_etg)$cors[2]
transit_cor <- svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit + EasyToGo, design = design_ordinal_norm_etg)$cors[2]
car_cor <- svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor + EasyToGo, design = design_ordinal_norm_etg)$cors[2]

filters <- c("Active", "Transit", "Car")
values <- c(active_cor, transit_cor, car_cor)
easytogocorr <- data.frame(Filter = filters, "EasyToGo" = values)
easytogocorr
```

## Declined Employment: Likert

```{r}
complete_data_de <- respondents_filtered %>% 
  filter(!is.na(DeclineEmployment), !is.na(employment_status)) %>%
  mutate(fexp_normalized = fexp_final / mean(fexp_final, na.rm = TRUE), 
         SA_pp_Active_unscaled = SA_pp_Active,
         SA_pp_Car_motor_unscaled = SA_pp_Car_motor,
         SA_pp_Transit_unscaled = SA_pp_Transit)


complete_data_scaled_de <- complete_data_de %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(vars_to_scale), 
                function(x) {
                  scaled <- scale(x)
                  return(as.numeric(scaled))
                }))

scale_centers_de <- sapply(vars_to_scale, function(var) {
  attr(scale(complete_data_de[[var]]), "scaled:center")
})

scale_scales_de <- sapply(vars_to_scale, function(var) {
  attr(scale(complete_data_de[[var]]), "scaled:scale")
})
```

```{r}
design_ordinal_norm_de <- svydesign(
  ids = ~1,
  weights = ~fexp_normalized,
  data = complete_data_scaled_de)
```

```{r}
DeclineEmployment_formula_3mode_ordinal_SA <- formula(update(ordinal_SA_pp_3mode_formula, DeclineEmployment_Likert ~ .))

DeclineEmployment_ordinal_3modes <- svyolr(DeclineEmployment_formula_3mode_ordinal_SA,
                                   design = design_ordinal_norm_de)

summary(DeclineEmployment_ordinal_3modes)
```

```{r}
active_cor <- svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Active + DeclineEmployment, design = design_ordinal_norm_de)$cors[2]
transit_cor <- svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Transit + DeclineEmployment, design = design_ordinal_norm_de)$cors[2]
car_cor <- svycor(~ Sf_dist_SA_pp_Car_motor + DeclineEmployment, design = design_ordinal_norm_de)$cors[2]


filters <- c("Active", "Transit", "Car")
values <- c(active_cor, transit_cor, car_cor)
declineemploymentcorr <- data.frame(Filter = filters, "DeclineEmployment" = values)

declineemploymentcorr
```

# Predicting probabilities

Using the estimated models, we simulate how changes in accessibility affect the predicted outcomes for different population profiles: an equity‑deserving group (low income, minority, disability, no car, etc.), an advantaged group, and a most common group. For each group we create a grid of accessibility values while holding all other controls at constant (median or chosen) values.

We then predict the continuous outcome (linear model) and the probability of each Likert category (ordinal model) using this grid, allowing us to visualize the effect of transit accessibility on perceived job accessibility.

```{r}
seq_val <- seq(from = 0,
                      to = round(1.10 * max(respondents$SA_pp_Transit, na.rm = TRUE),1),
                      by = 0.005)
```

Define profiles: 

```{r}
grid_disadvantaged <- as.data.frame(expand.grid("Mode" = c("Active","Transit","Car"),
                    "LongTermConditions" = "Yes",
                    "Woman_or_genderdiverse" = "Yes",
                    "Indigenous_or_racialized" = "Yes",
                    "CarNumber" = 0,
                    "BikeNumber" = 0,
                    "hh_income4" = "Less than $40,000",
                    "Temporary_or_refugee" = "Yes",
                    "hh_size" = 3, 
                    "Age" = 35,
                    "Match_mode" = "No",
                    "Transit_user" = "Yes",
                    "Active_user" = "No",
                    "pc_class" = "Small",
                    "TransitPass" = "Yes",
                    "TransitAvailable" = "Yes",
                    "Value" = seq_val))

grid_non_disadvantaged <- as.data.frame(expand.grid("Mode" = c("Active","Transit","Car"),
                    "LongTermConditions" = "No",
                    "Woman_or_genderdiverse" = "No",
                    "Indigenous_or_racialized" = "No",
                    "CarNumber" = 2,
                    "BikeNumber" = 1,
                    "hh_income4" = "More than $120,000",
                    "Temporary_or_refugee" = "No",
                    "hh_size" = 2, 
                    "Age" = 50,
                    "Match_mode" = "Yes",
                    "Transit_user" = "No",
                    "Active_user" = "No",
                    "pc_class" = "Large",
                    "TransitPass" = "No",
                    "TransitAvailable" = "Yes",
"Value" = seq_val))

grid_majority <- as.data.frame(expand.grid("Mode" = c("Active","Transit","Car"),
                    "LongTermConditions" = "No",
                    "Woman_or_genderdiverse" = "No",
                    "Indigenous_or_racialized" = "Yes",
                    "CarNumber" = 1,
                    "BikeNumber" = 1,
                    "hh_income4" = "$40,000 to $80,000",
                    "Temporary_or_refugee" = "No",
                    "hh_size" = 3, 
                    "Age" = 40,
                    "Match_mode" = "Yes",
                    "Transit_user" = "No",
                    "Active_user" = "No",
                    "pc_class" = "Large",
                    "TransitPass" = "No",
                    "TransitAvailable" = "Yes",
                    "Value" = seq_val))

grid_all_vars <- rbind(grid_disadvantaged, grid_non_disadvantaged, grid_majority)

controls_all_vars <- data.frame(SA_pp_Active_IV = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(respondents$SA_pp_Active_IV, weights = respondents$fexp_final, probs = 0.5), 
                         SA_pp_Transit_IV = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(respondents$SA_pp_Transit_IV, weights = respondents$fexp_final, probs = 0.5), 
                   SA_pp_Car_IV = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(respondents$SA_pp_Car_IV, weights = respondents$fexp_final, probs = 0.5),
                   SA_pp_Active = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(respondents$SA_pp_Active, weights = respondents$fexp_final, probs = 0.5),
                   SA_pp_Transit = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(respondents$SA_pp_Transit, weights = respondents$fexp_final, probs = 0.5),
                   SA_pp_Car_motor = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(respondents$SA_pp_Car_motor, weights = respondents$fexp_final, probs = 0.5), 
                   Pop_density = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(respondents$Pop_density, weights = respondents$fexp_final, probs = 0.5), 
                   SA_pp_Active_Res_IV = 0, 
                   SA_pp_Transit_Res_IV = 0, 
                   SA_pp_Car_Res_IV = 0)

grid_control_all_vars <- cbind(grid_all_vars, controls_all_vars)

cols_to_use <- c("LongTermConditions", "Woman_or_genderdiverse", "Indigenous_or_racialized", 
                 "CarNumber", "BikeNumber", "hh_income4", "hh_size", "Temporary_or_refugee", "Age", "Match_mode")

grid_control_all_vars_ready <- grid_control_all_vars %>%
  mutate(SA_pp_Active_IV = if_else(Mode == "Active", Value, SA_pp_Active_IV),
         SA_pp_Transit_IV = if_else(Mode == "Transit", Value, SA_pp_Transit_IV),
         SA_pp_Car_IV = if_else(Mode == "Car", Value, SA_pp_Car_IV), 
         SA_pp_Active = if_else(Mode == "Active", Value, SA_pp_Active),
         SA_pp_Transit = if_else(Mode == "Transit", Value, SA_pp_Transit),
         SA_pp_Car_motor = if_else(Mode == "Car", Value, SA_pp_Car_motor), 
         Case = pmap_chr(dplyr::select(., all_of(cols_to_use)), ~ {
           values <- map_chr(list(...), as.character)
           str_c(cols_to_use, ": ", values, collapse = " - ")}))
```

We loop over each profile and each mode, compute predictions from the linear and ordinal models, and store the results:

```{r}
predict_subset <- data.frame()
for(mode in unique(grid_control_all_vars_ready$Mode)){
for(grupo in unique(grid_control_all_vars_ready$Case)){
df <- grid_control_all_vars_ready %>%
filter(Case == grupo,
Mode == mode)
# # Travel Too Long
pred_ttl <- predict(TravelTooLong_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean,
newdata = df,
type = "response",
se.fit = TRUE)

pred_ttl <- as.data.frame(pred_ttl) %>%
rename(Probability = response) %>%
mutate(Outcome = "Travel too long",
Case = grupo,
Mode = mode)
pred_ttl$Value <- seq_val
# # Easy to reach
pred_etr <- predict(EasyToGo_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean,
newdata = df,
type = "response",
se.fit = TRUE)
pred_etr <- as.data.frame(pred_etr) %>%
rename(Probability = response) %>%
mutate(Outcome = "Easy to reach",
Case = grupo,
Mode = mode)
pred_etr$Value <- seq_val
# # Declined employment
pred_de <- predict(DeclineEmployment_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean,
newdata = df,
type = "response",
se.fit = TRUE)
pred_de <- as.data.frame(pred_de) %>%
rename(Probability = response) %>%
mutate(Outcome = "Declined employment",
Case = grupo,
Mode = mode)
pred_de$Value <- seq_val
# "Few jobs accessible"
pred_fj <- predict(FewJobs_3mode_lm_SA_pp_clean,
newdata = df,
type = "response",
se.fit = TRUE)
pred_fj <- as.data.frame(pred_fj) %>%
rename(Probability = response) %>%
mutate(Outcome = "Few jobs accessible",
Case = grupo,
Mode = mode)
pred_fj$Value <- seq_val
# Ordinal: Few jobs accessible
df_scaled_fj <- df

for(var in vars_to_scale){
df_scaled_fj[[var]] <- (df[[var]] - scale_centers_fj[var]) / scale_scales_fj[var]
}

pred_fj_ord <- predict(FewJobs_ordinal_3modes,
newdata = df_scaled_fj,
SE = TRUE,
type = "probs")
pred_fj_ord <- as.data.frame(pred_fj_ord)
pred_fj_ord$Value <- seq_val

pred_fj_ord <- pred_fj_ord %>%
pivot_longer(cols = c("Most Negative", "Negative", "Neutral","Positive", "Most Positive"),
names_to = "Likert", values_to = "Probability") %>%
mutate(Outcome = "Few jobs accessible - Ordinal",
Case = grupo,
Mode = mode)
# Ordinal: Easy to Reach
df_scaled_etg <- df
for(var in vars_to_scale){
df_scaled_etg[[var]] <- (df[[var]] - scale_centers_etg[var]) / scale_scales_etg[var]
}
pred_etr_ord <- predict(EasyToGo_ordinal_3modes,
newdata = df_scaled_etg,
SE = TRUE,
type = "probs")
pred_etr_ord <- as.data.frame(pred_etr_ord)
pred_etr_ord$Value <- seq_val
pred_etr_ord <- pred_etr_ord %>%
pivot_longer(cols = c("Most Negative", "Negative", "Neutral","Positive", "Most Positive"),
names_to = "Likert", values_to = "Probability") %>%
mutate(Outcome = "Easy to reach - Ordinal",
Case = grupo,
Mode = mode)
# Ordinal: Declined Employment
df_scaled_de <- df
for(var in vars_to_scale){
df_scaled_de[[var]] <- (df[[var]] - scale_centers_de[var]) / scale_scales_de[var]
}
pred_de_ord <- predict(DeclineEmployment_ordinal_3modes,
newdata = df_scaled_de,
SE = TRUE,
type = "probs")
pred_de_ord <- as.data.frame(pred_de_ord)
pred_de_ord$Value <- seq_val
pred_de_ord <- pred_de_ord %>%
pivot_longer(cols = c("Most Negative", "Negative", "Neutral","Positive", "Most Positive"),
names_to = "Likert", values_to = "Probability") %>%
mutate(Outcome = "Declined employment - Ordinal",
Case = grupo,
Mode = mode)
# Ordinal: Travel Too Long
df_scaled_ttg <- df
for(var in vars_to_scale){
df_scaled_ttg[[var]] <- (df[[var]] - scale_centers_ttg[var]) / scale_scales_ttg[var]
}
pred_ttg_ord <- predict(TravelTooLong_ordinal_3modes,
newdata = df_scaled_ttg,
SE = TRUE,
type = "probs")
pred_ttg_ord <- as.data.frame(pred_ttg_ord)
pred_ttg_ord$Value <- seq_val
pred_ttg_ord <- pred_ttg_ord %>%
pivot_longer(cols = c("Most Negative", "Negative", "Neutral","Positive", "Most Positive"),
names_to = "Likert", values_to = "Probability") %>%
mutate(Outcome = "Travel too long - Ordinal",
Case = grupo,
Mode = mode)
predict_subset <- predict_subset %>%
dplyr::bind_rows(pred_ttl,
pred_etr,
pred_de,
pred_fj,
pred_fj_ord,
pred_ttg_ord,
pred_etr_ord,
pred_de_ord)
}
}

rownames(predict_subset) <- NULL

predict_subset <- predict_subset %>%
mutate(Model = case_when(is.na(SE) ~ "Ordinal",
TRUE ~ "Linear"))

# Comment to the co-authors: the format is weird because I had to recover this code from the `History` after not properly saving the file! :/ 
```

```{r}
#predict_subset <- readRDS(paste0(diretorio_export,"predictions.rds"))
```

# Visualizing probabilities

```{r}
## Most disadvantaged case
LongTermConditions_dis <- unique(grid_disadvantaged$LongTermConditions)
Woman_or_genderdiverse_dis <- unique(grid_disadvantaged$Woman_or_genderdiverse)
Indigenous_or_racialized_dis <- unique(grid_disadvantaged$Indigenous_or_racialized)
CarNumber_dis <- unique(grid_disadvantaged$CarNumber)
BikeNumber_dis <- unique(grid_disadvantaged$BikeNumber)
hh_income4_dis <- unique(grid_disadvantaged$hh_income4)
hh_size_dis <- unique(grid_disadvantaged$hh_size)
Temporary_or_refugee_dis <- unique(grid_disadvantaged$Temporary_or_refugee)
Age_dis <- unique(grid_disadvantaged$Age)
Match_mode_dis <- unique(grid_disadvantaged$Match_mode)

priority_group <- paste0("LongTermConditions: ", LongTermConditions_dis ," - Woman_or_genderdiverse: ", Woman_or_genderdiverse_dis ," - Indigenous_or_racialized: ", Indigenous_or_racialized_dis ," - CarNumber: ", CarNumber_dis ," - BikeNumber: ", BikeNumber_dis ," - hh_income4: ", hh_income4_dis ," - hh_size: ",  hh_size_dis," - Temporary_or_refugee: ", Temporary_or_refugee_dis ," - Age: ", Age_dis," - Match_mode: ", Match_mode_dis)

priority_group
```

```{r}
## Most non-disadvantaged case
LongTermConditions_ndis <- unique(grid_non_disadvantaged$LongTermConditions)
Woman_or_genderdiverse_ndis <- unique(grid_non_disadvantaged$Woman_or_genderdiverse)
Indigenous_or_racialized_ndis <- unique(grid_non_disadvantaged$Indigenous_or_racialized)
CarNumber_ndis <- unique(grid_non_disadvantaged$CarNumber)
BikeNumber_ndis <- unique(grid_non_disadvantaged$BikeNumber)
hh_income4_ndis <- unique(grid_non_disadvantaged$hh_income4)
hh_size_ndis <- unique(grid_non_disadvantaged$hh_size)
Temporary_or_refugee_ndis <- unique(grid_non_disadvantaged$Temporary_or_refugee)
Age_ndis <- unique(grid_non_disadvantaged$Age)
Match_mode_ndis <- unique(grid_non_disadvantaged$Match_mode)

non_priority_group <- paste0("LongTermConditions: ", LongTermConditions_ndis ," - Woman_or_genderdiverse: ", Woman_or_genderdiverse_ndis ," - Indigenous_or_racialized: ", Indigenous_or_racialized_ndis ," - CarNumber: ", CarNumber_ndis ," - BikeNumber: ", BikeNumber_ndis ," - hh_income4: ", hh_income4_ndis ," - hh_size: ",  hh_size_ndis," - Temporary_or_refugee: ", Temporary_or_refugee_ndis ," - Age: ", Age_ndis," - Match_mode: ", Match_mode_ndis)

non_priority_group
```

```{r}
## Majority pop
LongTermConditions_maj <- unique(grid_majority$LongTermConditions)  
Woman_or_genderdiverse_maj <- unique(grid_majority$Woman_or_genderdiverse)
Indigenous_or_racialized_maj <- unique(grid_majority$Indigenous_or_racialized)
CarNumber_maj <- unique(grid_majority$CarNumber)
BikeNumber_maj <- unique(grid_majority$BikeNumber)
hh_income4_maj <- unique(grid_majority$hh_income4)
hh_size_maj <- unique(grid_majority$hh_size)
Temporary_or_refugee_maj <- unique(grid_majority$Temporary_or_refugee)
Age_maj <- unique(grid_majority$Age)
Match_mode_maj <- unique(grid_majority$Match_mode)

majority_group <- paste0("LongTermConditions: ", LongTermConditions_maj ," - Woman_or_genderdiverse: ", Woman_or_genderdiverse_maj ," - Indigenous_or_racialized: ", Indigenous_or_racialized_maj ," - CarNumber: ", CarNumber_maj ," - BikeNumber: ", BikeNumber_maj ," - hh_income4: ", hh_income4_maj ," - hh_size: ",  hh_size_maj," - Temporary_or_refugee: ", Temporary_or_refugee_maj ," - Age: ", Age_maj," - Match_mode: ", Match_mode_maj) 

majority_group
```

```{r}
accessibility_lines <- data.frame(Statistic = c("Median", "Maximum"),
  Value = c(Hmisc::wtd.quantile(respondents$SA_pp_Transit, weights = respondents$fexp_final, probs = 0.5), 
            max(respondents$SA_pp_Transit, na.rm = TRUE)))

median_sa <- Hmisc::wtd.quantile(respondents$SA_pp_Transit, weights = respondents$fexp_final, probs = 0.5)
max_sa <- max(respondents$SA_pp_Transit, na.rm = TRUE)
mean_sa <- Hmisc::wtd.mean(respondents$SA_pp_Transit, weights = respondents$fexp_final)
```

```{r}
outcome_order_linear <- c("Travel too long", 
                   "Easy to reach", 
                   "Declined employment", 
                   "Few jobs accessible")
my_colors_linear <- c("#66c2a5","#fc8d62","#8da0cb","#e78ac3")

names(my_colors_linear) <- outcome_order_linear

Likert_order <- c("Most Negative", "Negative", "Neutral", "Positive", "Most Positive")

my_colors_likert <- c("#d7191c", "#fdae61", "#bababa", "#a6d96a", "#1a9641")
names(my_colors_likert) <- Likert_order
```

```{r}
priority_group_name <- "Equity-deserving"
non_priority_group_name <- "Advantaged group"
majority_group_name <- "Most common population"
```

```{r}
predict_subset <- predict_subset %>%
  mutate(Equity = case_when(Case == priority_group ~
                              priority_group_name,
Case == non_priority_group ~ non_priority_group_name,
Case == majority_group ~ majority_group_name),
Equity = factor(Equity, levels = c(priority_group_name, majority_group_name, non_priority_group_name)))

predict_subset_linear <- predict_subset %>%
filter(Model == "Linear", Outcome == "Few jobs accessible")

predict_subset_ordinal <- predict_subset %>%
filter(Model == "Ordinal", Outcome == "Few jobs accessible - Ordinal") %>%
mutate(Probability = Probability * 100)

predict_subset_standads_ordinal_fj <- predict_subset_ordinal %>%
filter(Mode == "Transit",
Case %in% c(priority_group, non_priority_group, majority_group)) %>%
mutate(Likert = factor(Likert, levels = Likert_order))
```

## Linear cases

For Linear models, we plot the predicted probability (0 to 100) against transit accessibility, with confidence bands, faceted by equity group.

```{r}
predict_subset_standads_linear <- predict_subset %>% 
  filter(Mode == "Transit", 
         Case %in% c(priority_group, non_priority_group, majority_group))

probabilities_transit_linear <- predict_subset_standads_linear %>%   
  ggplot(aes(x = Value, y = Probability, color = Outcome, fill = Outcome)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.75, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Probability - SE, ymax = Probability + SE), 
              alpha = 0.10, linetype = 0) +  
  geom_vline(data = accessibility_lines,
             aes(xintercept = Value),
             linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.75, linewidth = 0.3, color = "#666666") +
  annotate("text", x = median_sa, y = 97, 
           label = paste0("Median: ", round(median_sa,2)), 
           angle = 90, 
           hjust = 1, vjust = -0.5, size = 2.8, color = "#333333") +
  annotate("text", x = max_sa, y = 97, 
           label = paste0("Max: ", round(max_sa,2)), 
           angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = -0.5, size = 2.8, color = "#333333") +
  labs(x = "Number of jobs reached by public transit per person",
    y = "Predicted probability",
    title = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 100), 
                  xlim = c(0, 1.10 * max(respondents$SA_pp_Transit, na.rm = TRUE))) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  facet_wrap(~Equity, ncol = 2)  +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_colors_linear) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors_linear) + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",         
    legend.direction = "horiozontal",    
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9))

ggsave(file = paste0(diretorio_export_images,"/linear/accessibility-effect-transit.jpg"),
       plot = probabilities_transit_linear,
       width = 15,
       height = 9,
       units = "cm",
       dpi = 300)

knitr::include_graphics(paste0(diretorio_export_images,"/linear/accessibility-effect-transit.jpg"))
```

```{r}
for(caso in unique(predict_subset_linear$Case)){
  probabilities_sig <- predict_subset_linear %>% 
  filter(Case == caso) %>% 
  mutate(Outcome = factor(Outcome, levels = outcome_order_linear)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Value, y = Probability, color = Outcome, fill = Outcome)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.75) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Probability - SE, ymax = Probability + SE), 
              alpha = 0.15, linetype = 0) +  
  labs(x = "Number of jobs reached",
    y = "Predicted probability",
    title = str_wrap(caso, width = 70)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 100)) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  facet_wrap(~ Mode, ncol = 3)  +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_colors_linear) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors_linear) + 
  theme(#legend.position = "bottom",         
    legend.direction = "vertical",    
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9))

ggsave(file =  paste0(diretorio_export_images, "linear/sig/", caso,".jpg"),
       plot = probabilities_sig,
       width = 18,
       height = 7,
       units = "cm",
       dpi = 300)
}
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(diretorio_export_images,"/linear/sig/", priority_group,".jpg"))
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(diretorio_export_images,"/linear/sig/", non_priority_group,".jpg"))
knitr::include_graphics(paste0(diretorio_export_images,"/linear/sig/", majority_group,".jpg"))
```

## Ordinal cases

### Few jobs
